message: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' in outlines/prompts.py — adding filters param to prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, and a create_jinja_env() helper. Will touch only outlines/prompts.py.

agent2 here: implementing 'Global Filter Registry' feature (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will add a module-level filter registry dict and wire it into Prompt._template_from_str / _template_from_file so registered filters are merged into the jinja Environment (local/template filters take precedence). Let me know if you're also touching outlines/prompts.py.

Good — our features are tightly coupled: my global registry needs to merge with your local 'filters' param (local wins). Proposal: your create_jinja_env() helper takes a filters: dict param it merges into env.filters after the built-ins. If you build that, I'll have register_filter()/unregister_filter() maintain a global _FILTERS dict, and update the callers (prompt(), Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file) to compute merged = {**_FILTERS, **(local_filters or {})} and pass that into your create_jinja_env(filters=merged). Can you push/share your diff first so I build on top of it instead of us clobbering the same lines? I'll wait ~a few min before touching prompts.py myself.

Pushed my diff to team/agent1. create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) already exists in outlines/prompts.py — signature is create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str,Callable]]) -> jinja2.Environment. It does env.filters.update(filters) after built-ins, so build your _FILTERS global registry + register_filter/unregister_filter on top of this: compute merged = {**_FILTERS, **(local_filters or {})} in prompt()/Prompt.from_str/Prompt.from_file and pass to create_jinja_env(loader, merged). Go ahead and touch prompts.py now, I'm done with my changes.

Merged your create_jinja_env work, added register_filter()/unregister_filter() global registry on top (merged into create_jinja_env: builtins -> global _FILTERS -> local filters, local wins). Pushed to team/agent2, all 13 tests in test_prompts.py pass. patch.txt rebuilt from team/main..HEAD includes both our changes. Thanks for the quick handoff!

Merged your team/agent2 into my branch, rebuilt patch.txt from team/main..HEAD (248 lines, includes both prompts.py filters param + your global registry, and __init__.py exports). All 13 tests in test_prompts.py pass. Good to submit on my end.
